overview.title: Inventory Integration — Overview
overview.description: The Inventory Integration keeps stock and prices automatically synchronized between your ERP/WMS and the Rappi catalog, preventing stockouts and price inconsistencies.

sections.title: Prerequisite for Open Orders
sections.content: Inventory Integration is **required** before implementing Open Orders. Without it, the stock shown on the platform may not reflect reality, leading to cancellations due to out-of-stock products.

items.title: Via API
items.text: Real-time updates via REST calls. Ideal for systems with HTTP integration capability.

items.title: Via SFTP
items.text: CSV/JSON file uploads at scheduled intervals. Ideal for ERPs with SFTP support.

sections.title: Which method should I choose?
sections.content:
Use **API** when:
- Your system can make real-time HTTP calls
- You need instant stock updates
- You have technical resources for REST integration

Use **SFTP** when:
- Your ERP generates export files but doesn't support HTTP calls
- Periodic updates (e.g., every hour) are sufficient for your operation
- You prefer a file-based approach over real-time integration

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: The Inventory API uses the same OAuth 2.0 system as the other Rappi APIs.

sections.title: Same credentials
sections.content: Use the same `client_id` and `client_secret` credentials. See the **Authentication** section in Open Orders for the full token acquisition process.

methods.title: Integration Methods
methods.description: Choose between API integration (real-time) or SFTP (periodic files).

update-stock.title: Update Stock
update-stock.description: Updates the availability and stock quantity of a specific product.

rows.name: available
rows.description: Whether the product is available for sale.

rows.name: quantity
rows.description: Stock quantity. If 0, `available` is automatically set to `false`.

update-price.title: Update Price
update-price.description: Updates the sale price of a product.

rows.name: promotional_price
rows.description: Promotional price in cents (must be less than `price`).

bulk-update.title: Bulk Update
bulk-update.description: Updates stock and prices for multiple products in a single request. Supports up to 500 items per call.

sections.title: Recommended for periodic sync
sections.content: Use the bulk endpoint for synchronizations during peak hours (e.g., store opening, restocking).

sections.title: Request Example
Response (200 OK)

sftp-overview.title: SFTP Overview
sftp-overview.description: SFTP integration allows ERP systems that don't support HTTP calls to sync inventory via file uploads.

sections.title: SFTP Credentials
sections.content: SFTP access credentials are provided separately from OAuth credentials. Request them from your Integrations Specialist during onboarding.

sftp-format.title: SFTP File Format
sftp-format.description: Specification of the inventory file formats accepted via SFTP.

rows.name: promotional_price
rows.description: Promotional price in cents (leave empty if none).

sftp-schedule.title: SFTP Scheduling
sftp-schedule.description: Configuration for SFTP file processing frequency.

sections.title: Processing Frequency
sections.content:
Files deposited via SFTP are processed every **15 minutes**. For more frequent updates, use the API integration.

The file must be named in the format `inventory_{YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS}.csv` to facilitate tracking.

sections.title: Full file vs Delta
sections.content: Always send the **complete file** with all store SKUs, not just changes (delta). Items missing from the file are not automatically changed.
